text.title: Versioned Agent-Memory Files (decisions / patterns / mistakes) Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Memory bundle contains exactly four files: decisions.md, patterns.md, mistakes.md, session.md. No more, no less.
rule.rationale: More files dilute the agent's attention; fewer collapse semantics.

rule.statement: Memory files are committed to the repo (or workspace .aidocs/memory/) — not stored in chat scratchpad alone.
rule.rationale: Chat resets; committed files survive and are auditable.

rule.statement: decisions.md is append-only with timestamped entries; superseded decisions are marked, not deleted.
rule.rationale: Decision audit trail is the value; deletion loses history.

rule.statement: session.md holds current focus / blockers / open questions; rotates per major session boundary.
rule.rationale: Session state is volatile; keeping it bounded prevents the file from becoming the whole agent history.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md / agent config MUST load all 4 memory files on session start.
rule.rationale: Memory invisible to the agent is no memory.

rule.statement: If project is throwaway / one-off OR files would leak secrets OR team rejects committed AI artefacts, STOP.
rule.rationale: Forced memory in unsuitable contexts is overhead.
